req.title: Patient portal login (boundary)
req.statement: A patient shall log in using SSO; failed attempts shall be rate-limited and notify the patient. Boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements must be tested.

tests.name: naive_case
tests.executable:
# Pytest Tests: SSO Login with Rate Limiting and Notifications

```python
"""
Test Suite: SSO Login with Rate Limiting and Patient Notifications
Requirement: A patient shall log in using SSO; failed attempts shall be
             rate-limited and notify the patient.
             Boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements must be tested.

Author: Quality Engineer
"""

import time
import pytest
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, call
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta
from freezegun import freeze_time

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Assumed application interfaces (replace with real imports in your project)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# from app.auth.sso import SSOAuthenticator
# from app.auth.rate_limiter import RateLimiter
# from app.notifications import NotificationService
# from app.models import Patient, LoginAttempt
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


# ===========================================================================
# Fixtures
# ===========================================================================

@pytest.fixture
def mock_sso_provider():
    """Simulates an external SSO identity provider."""
    provider = MagicMock()
    provider.validate_token.return_value = True
    provider.get_patient_claims.return_value = {
        "sub": "patient-uuid-001",
        "email": "patient@example.com",
        "iat": int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),
        "exp": int((datetime.now(timezone.utc) + timedelta(hours=1)).timestamp()),
    }
    return provider


@pytest.fixture
def mock_notification_service():
    """Simulates the notification service (email/SMS)."""
    return MagicMock()


@pytest.fixture
def mock_patient_repository():
    """Simulates the patient data store."""
    repo = MagicMock()
    repo.find_by_sso_sub.return_value = MagicMock(
        id="patient-uuid-001",
        email="patient@example.com",
        is_active=True,
        failed_attempts=0,
        locked_until=None,
    )
    return repo


@pytest.fixture
def rate_limiter(mock_patient_repository):
    """
    In-memory rate limiter stub.
    Replace with your real RateLimiter instantiation.
    """
    from collections import defaultdict

    class InMemoryRateLimiter:
        MAX_ATTEMPTS = 5          # boundary value under test
        WINDOW_SECONDS = 300      # 5-minute rolling window
        LOCKOUT_SECONDS = 900     # 15-minute lockout

        def __init__(self):
            self._attempts: dict = defaultdict(list)  # patient_id -> [timestamp]
            self._lockouts: dict = {}                  # patient_id -> unlock_time

        def record_failure(self, patient_id: str, timestamp: float) -> None:
            self._attempts[patient_id].append(timestamp)
            window_start = timestamp - self.WINDOW_SECONDS
            self._attempts[patient_id] = [
                t for t in self._attempts[patient_id] if t >= window_start
            ]
            if len(self._attempts[patient_id]) >= self.MAX_ATTEMPTS:
                self._lockouts[patient_id] = timestamp + self.LOCKOUT_SECONDS

        def is_locked(self, patient_id: str, current_time: float) -> bool:
            unlock_at = self._lockouts.get(patient_id)
            if unlock_at is None:
                return False
            return current_time < unlock_at

        def unlock_time(self, patient_id: str) -> float | None:
            return self._lockouts.get(patient_id)

        def attempt_count(self, patient_id: str, current_time: float) -> int:
            window_start = current_time - self.WINDOW_SECONDS
            return sum(
                1 for t in self._attempts[patient_id] if t >= window_start
            )

        def reset(self, patient_id: str) -> None:
            self._attempts.pop(patient_id, None)
            self._lockouts.pop(patient_id, None)

    return InMemoryRateLimiter()


@pytest.fixture
def auth_service(mock_sso_provider, mock_notification_service,
                 mock_patient_repository, rate_limiter):
    """
    Minimal SSOAuthenticator stub wiring all collaborators together.
    Replace with your real SSOAuthenticator.
    """

    class SSOAuthService:
        def __init__(self, provider, notifier, repo, limiter):
            self.provider = provider
            self.notifier = notifier
            self.repo = repo
            self.limiter = limiter

        def login(self, token: str, request_time: float = None) -> dict:
            now = request_time or time.time()
            patient_id = None

            try:
                claims = self.provider.validate_and_decode(token)
                patient_id = claims["sub"]
            except Exception:
                return {"success": False, "reason": "invalid_token"}

            # Check lockout BEFORE processing
            if self.limiter.is_locked(patient_id, now):
                unlock_at = self.limiter.unlock_time(patient_id)
                self.notifier.send_lockout_reminder(patient_id)
                return {
                    "success": False,
                    "reason": "rate_limited",
                    "unlock_at": unlock_at,
                }

            # Validate token with SSO provider
            valid = self.provider.validate_token(token)
            patient = self.repo.find_by_sso_sub(patient_id)

            if not valid or patient is None or not patient.is_active:
                self.limiter.record_failure(patient_id, now)
                count = self.limiter.attempt_count(patient_id, now)
                self.notifier.send_failed_attempt(patient_id, count)

                if self.limiter.is_locked(patient_id, now):
                    self.notifier.send_account_locked(patient_id)
                    return {
                        "success": False,
                        "reason": "rate_limited",
                        "unlock_at": self.limiter.unlock_time(patient_id),
                    }
                return {"success": False, "reason": "authentication_failed"}

            # Success — clear any previous failures
            self.limiter.reset(patient_id)
            return {"success": True, "patient_id": patient_id}

    # Wire provider to also expose validate_and_decode
    mock_sso_provider.validate_and_decode.return_value = \
        mock_sso_provider.get_patient_claims()

    return SSOAuthService(
        mock_sso_provider,
        mock_notification_service,
        mock_patient_repository,
        rate_limiter,
    )


PATIENT_ID = "patient-uuid-001"
BASE_TIME = 1_700_000_000.0   # fixed epoch reference for boundary tests


# ===========================================================================
# TC-SSO-001: Successful SSO Login
# ===========================================================================

class TestSuccessfulLogin:

    def test_valid_token_returns_success(self, auth_service):
        result = auth_service.login("valid-sso-token", BASE_TIME)
        assert result["success"] is True

    def test_successful_login_contains_patient_id(self, auth_service):
        result = auth_service.login("valid-sso-token", BASE_TIME)
        assert result["patient_id"] == PATIENT_ID

    def test_successful_login_clears_previous_failures(
            self, auth_service, rate_limiter):
        # Seed two prior failures
        rate_limiter.record_failure(PATIENT_ID, BASE_TIME - 60)
        rate_limiter.record_failure(PATIENT_ID, BASE_TIME - 30)
        assert rate_limiter.attempt_count(PATIENT_ID, BASE_TIME) == 2

        auth_service.login("valid-sso-token", BASE_TIME)
        assert rate_limiter.attempt_count(PATIENT_ID, BASE_TIME) == 0

    def test_successful_login_sends_no_failure_notification(
            self, auth_service, mock_notification_service):
        auth_service.login("valid-sso-token", BASE_TIME)
        mock_notification_service.send_failed_attempt.assert_not_called()
        mock_notification_service.send_account_locked.assert_not_called()


# ===========================================================================
# TC-SSO-002: Single Failed Attempt
# ===========================================================================

class TestSingleFailedAttempt:

    @pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
    def force_invalid(self, mock_sso_provider):
        mock_sso_provider.validate_token.return_value = False

    def test_returns_authentication_failed(self, auth_service):
        result = auth_service.login("bad-token", BASE_TIME)
        assert result["success"] is False
        assert result["reason"] == "authentication_failed"

    def test_notification_sent_on_first_failure(
            self, auth_service, mock_notification_service):
        auth_service.login("bad-token", BASE_TIME)
        mock_notification_service.send_failed_attempt.assert_called_once_with(
            PATIENT_ID, 1
        )

    def test_failure_count_increments(self, auth_service, rate_limiter):
        auth_service.login("bad-token", BASE_TIME)
        assert rate_limiter.attempt_count(PATIENT_ID, BASE_TIME) == 1

    def test_no_lockout_on_single_failure(self, auth_service, rate_limiter):
        auth_service.login("bad-token", BASE_TIME)
        assert rate_limiter.is_locked(PATIENT_ID, BASE_TIME) is False


# ===========================================================================
# TC-SSO-003: Rate Limiting — Boundary Values on Attempt Count
# ===========================================================================

class TestRateLimitingBoundaryValues:
    """
    MAX_ATTEMPTS = 5
    Boundary values: 0, 1, 4 (MAX-1), 5 (MAX), 6 (MAX+1)
    """

    @pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
    def force_invalid(self, mock_sso_provider):
        mock_sso_provider.validate_token.return_value = False

    def _make_failures(self, auth_service, count: int, start_time: float):
        for i in range(count):
            auth_service.login("bad-token", start_time + i)

    # --- Attempt 0 (no history) ---
    def test_zero_attempts_not_locked(self, rate_limiter):
        assert rate_limiter.is_locked(PATIENT_ID, BASE_TIME) is False

    # --- Attempt 1 (MIN boundary) ---
    def test_one_attempt_not_locked(self, auth_service, rate_limiter):
        self._make_failures(auth_service, 1, BASE_TIME)
        assert rate_limiter.is_locked(PATIENT_ID, BASE_TIME + 1) is False

    # --- Attempt MAX-1 = 4 (just below threshold) ---
    def test_four_attempts_not_yet_locked(self, auth_service, rate_limiter):
        self._make_failures(auth_service, 4, BASE_TIME)
        assert rate_limiter.is_locked(
            PATIENT_ID, BASE_TIME + 4) is False

    def test_four_attempts_notification_count_correct(
            self, auth_service, mock_notification_service):
        self._make_failures(auth_service, 4, BASE_TIME)
        calls = mock_notification_service.send_failed_attempt.call_args_list
        assert len(calls) == 4
        # Verify incremental counts 1..4
        for idx, c in enumerate(calls, start=1):
            assert c == call(PATIENT_ID, idx)

    # --- Attempt MAX = 5 (exact threshold — lockout triggered) ---
    def test_five_attempts_triggers_lockout(self, auth_service, rate_limiter):
        self._make_failures(auth_service, 5, BASE_TIME)
        assert rate_limiter.is_locked(PATIENT_ID, BASE_TIME + 5) is True

    def test_fifth_attempt_returns_rate_limited(self, auth_service):
        self._make_failures(auth_service, 4, BASE_TIME)
        result = auth_service.login("bad-token", BASE_TIME + 4)
        assert result["reason"] == "rate_limited"

    def test_lockout_notification_sent_on_fifth_attempt(
            self, auth_service, mock_notification_service):
        self._make_failures(auth_service, 5, BASE_TIME)
        mock_notification_service.send_account_locked.assert_called_once_with(
            PATIENT_ID
        )

    # --- Attempt MAX+1 = 6 (beyond threshold) ---
    def test_sixth_attempt_still_rate_limited(self, auth_service, rate_limiter):
        self._make_failures(auth_service, 5, BASE_TIME)
        # Attempt after lockout is in effect
        result = auth_service.login("bad-token", BASE_TIME + 10)
        assert result["reason"] == "rate_limited"

    def test_lockout_reminder_sent_on_post_lockout_attempt(
            self, auth_service, mock_notification_service):
        self._make_failures(auth_service, 5, BASE_TIME)
        mock_notification_service.reset_mock()
        auth_service.login("bad-token", BASE_TIME + 10)
        mock_notification_service.send_lockout_reminder.assert_called_once()


# ===========================================================================
# TC-SSO-004: Rate Limiting — Timestamp Boundary Values
# ===========================================================================

class TestRateLimitingTimestampBoundaries:
    """
    WINDOW_SECONDS  = 300   (5 minutes)
    LOCKOUT_SECONDS = 900   (15 minutes)

    Boundary timestamps relative to the window start:
      - exactly at window edge (t = window_start)
      - one second before expiry
      - one second after expiry
    """

    @pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
    def force_invalid(self, mock_sso_provider):
        mock_sso_provider.validate_token.return_value = False

    WINDOW = 300
    LOCKOUT = 900
    MAX = 5

    def _seed_failures(self, rate_limiter, count, timestamps):
        for ts in timestamps:
            rate_limiter.record_failure(PATIENT_ID, ts)

    # --- Window boundary: failure exactly at window start ---
    def test_failure_at_exact_window_start_is_counted(self, rate_limiter):
        window_start = BASE_TIME
        rate_limiter.record_failure(PATIENT_ID, window_start)
        assert rate_limiter.attempt_count(PATIENT_ID, window_start) == 1

    # --- Window boundary: failure one second before window start is excluded ---
    def test_failure_one_second_before_window_is_excluded(self, rate_limiter):
        now = BASE_TIME + self.WINDOW
        old_ts = BASE_TIME - 1   # outside the 300s window
        rate_limiter.record_failure(PATIENT_ID, old_ts)
        assert rate_limiter.attempt_count(PATIENT_ID, now) == 0

    # --- Window boundary: failure one second inside window is included ---
    def test_failure_one_second_inside_window_is_included(self, rate_limiter):
        now = BASE_TIME + self.WINDOW
        edge_ts = BASE_TIME  # exactly at window_start = now - WINDOW
        rate_limiter.record_failure(PATIENT_ID, edge_ts)
        assert rate_limiter.attempt_count(PATIENT_ID, now) == 1

    # --- 4 stale + 1 fresh should NOT trigger lockout ---
    def test_four_stale_one_fresh_no_lockout(
            self, auth_service, rate_limiter, mock_sso_provider):
        stale_time = BASE_TIME - self.WINDOW - 1   # outside window
        for _ in range(4):
            rate_limiter.record_failure(PATIENT_ID, stale_time)
        # Now one fresh failure within window
        auth_service.login("bad-token", BASE_TIME)
        assert rate_limiter.is_locked(PATIENT_ID, BASE_TIME) is False

    # --- Lockout expiry: still locked one second before unlock ---
    def test_still_locked_one_second_before_unlock(
            self, auth_service, rate_limiter):
        for i in range(self.MAX):
            rate_limiter.record_failure(PATIENT_ID, BASE_TIME + i)
        unlock_at = rate_limiter.unlock_time(PATIENT_ID)
        assert rate_limiter.is_locked(PATIENT_ID, unlock_at - 1) is True

    # --- Lockout expiry: exactly at unlock time (boundary — not locked) ---
    def test_not_locked_at_exact_unlock_time(self, auth_service, rate_limiter):
        for i in range(self.MAX):
            rate_limiter.record_failure(PATIENT_ID, BASE_TIME + i)
        unlock_at = rate_limiter.unlock_time(PATIENT_ID)
        # At exactly unlock_at the lock should have expired
        assert rate_limiter.is_locked(PATIENT_ID, unlock_at) is False

    # --- Lockout expiry: one second after unlock ---
    def test_not_locked_one_second_after_unlock(self, auth_service, rate_limiter):
        for i in range(self.MAX):
            rate_limiter.record_failure(PATIENT_ID, BASE_TIME + i)
        unlock_at = rate_limiter.unlock_time(PATIENT_ID)
        assert rate_limiter.is_locked(PATIENT_ID, unlock_at + 1) is False

    # --- Lockout duration is exactly LOCKOUT_SECONDS ---
    def test_lockout_duration_is_correct(self, rate_limiter):
        for i in range(self.MAX):
            rate_limiter.record_failure(PATIENT_ID, BASE_TIME + i)
        unlock_at = rate_limiter.unlock_time(PATIENT_ID)
        last_failure_time = BASE_TIME + (self.MAX - 1)
        assert unlock_at == pytest.approx(
            last_failure_time + self.LOCKOUT, abs=1
        )

    # --- Token issued-at (iat) exactly now is accepted ---
    def test_token_iat_exactly_now_is_accepted(
            self, auth_service, mock_sso_provider):
        now = int(BASE_TIME)
        mock_sso_provider.get_patient_claims.return_value = {
            "sub": PATIENT_ID,
            "email": "patient@example.com",
            "iat": now,
            "exp": now + 3600,
        }
        mock_sso_provider.validate_and_decode.return_value = \
            mock_sso_provider.get_patient_claims()
        result = auth_service.login("valid-token", BASE_TIME)
        assert result["success"] is True

    # --- Token issued-at (iat) in the future should fail ---
    def test_token_iat_in_future_is_rejected(
            self, auth_service, mock_sso_provider):
        future_iat = int(BASE_TIME) + 3600
        mock_sso_provider.validate_token.return_value = False   # provider rejects
        mock_sso_provider.get_patient_claims.return_value = {
            "sub": PATIENT_ID,
            "email": "patient@example.com",
            "iat": future_iat,
            "exp": future_iat + 3600,
        }
        mock_sso_provider.validate_and_decode.return_value = \
            mock_sso_provider.get_patient_claims()
        result = auth_service.login("future-token", BASE_TIME)
        assert result["success"] is False

    # --- Token exp at exactly now (boundary — expired) ---
    def test_token_exp_exactly_now_is_rejected(
            self, auth_service, mock_sso_provider):
        now = int(BASE_TIME)
        mock_sso_provider.validate_token.return_value = False  # expired
        mock_sso_provider.get_patient_claims.return_value = {
            "sub": PATIENT_ID,
            "email": "patient@example.com",
            "iat": now - 3600,
            "exp": now,          # expires exactly now
        }
        mock_sso_provider.validate_and_decode.return_value = \
            mock_sso_provider.get_patient_claims()
        result = auth_service.login("expired-token", BASE_TIME)
        assert result["success"] is False

    # --- Token exp one second in future is accepted ---
    def test_token_exp_one_second_in_future_is_accepted(
            self, auth_service, mock_sso_provider):
        now = int(BASE_TIME)
        mock_sso_provider.validate_token.return_value = True
        mock_sso_provider.get_patient_claims.return_value = {
            "sub": PATIENT_ID,
            "email": "patient@example.com",
            "iat": now - 3600,
            "exp": now + 1,
        }
        mock_sso_provider.validate_and_decode.return_value = \
            mock_sso_provider.get_patient_claims()
        result = auth_service.login("nearly-expired-token", BASE_TIME)
        assert result["success"] is True


# ===========================================================================
# TC-SSO-005: Notifications
# ===========================================================================

class TestNotifications:

    @pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
    def force_invalid(self, mock_sso_provider):
        mock_sso_provider.validate_token.return_value = False

    def test_notification_includes_correct_attempt_number(
            self, auth_service, mock_notification_service):
        for expected_count in range(1, 5):
            auth_service.login("bad-token", BASE_TIME + expected_count)
            mock_notification_service.send_failed_attempt.assert_called_with(
                PATIENT_ID, expected_count
            )

    def test_lockout_notification_sent_exactly_once_on_threshold(
            self, auth_service, mock_notification_service):
        for i in range(5):
            auth_service.login("bad-token", BASE_TIME + i)
        assert mock_notification_service.send_account_locked.call_count == 1

    def test_no_duplicate_lockout_notification_after_threshold(
            self, auth_service, mock_notification_service):
        for i in range(7):
            auth_service.login("bad-token", BASE_TIME + i)
        # send_account_locked must fire exactly once (on attempt 5)
        assert mock_notification_service.send_account_locked.call_count == 1

    def test_lockout_reminder_not_sent_before_lockout(
            self, auth_service, mock_notification_service):
        for i in range(4):
            auth_service.login("bad-token", BASE_TIME + i)
        mock_notification_service.send_lockout_reminder.assert_not_called()

    def test_lockout_notification_contains_patient_id(
            self, auth_service, mock_notification_service):
        for i in range(5):
            auth_service.login("bad-token", BASE_TIME + i)
        args, _ = mock_notification_service.send_account_locked.call_args
        assert args[0] == PATIENT_ID


# ===========================================================================
# TC-SSO-006: Inactive / Disabled Patient Account
# ===========================================================================

class TestInactivePatient:

    def test_inactive_patient_cannot_login(
            self, auth_service, mock_patient_repository):
        mock_patient_repository.find_by_sso_sub.return_value = MagicMock(
            id=PATIENT_ID, is_active=False
        )
        result = auth_service.login("valid-token", BASE_TIME)
        assert result["success"] is False

    def test_inactive_patient_increments_failure_count(
            self, auth_service, mock_patient_repository, rate_limiter):
        mock_patient_repository.find_by_sso_sub.return_value = MagicMock(
            id=PATIENT_ID, is_active=False
        )
        auth_service.login("valid-token", BASE_TIME)
        assert rate_limiter.attempt_count(PATIENT_ID, BASE_TIME) == 1


# ===========================================================================
# TC-SSO-007: Post-Lockout Recovery
# ===========================================================================

class TestPostLockoutRecovery:

    @pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
    def force_invalid(self, mock_sso_provider):
        mock_sso_provider.validate_token.return_value = False

    def test_successful_login_after_lockout_expires(
            self, auth_service, rate_limiter, mock_sso_provider):
        # Trigger lockout
        for i in range(5):
            auth_service.login("bad-token", BASE_TIME + i)

        unlock_at = rate_limiter.unlock_time(PATIENT_ID)
        # Reset mock to allow success
        mock_sso_provider.validate_token.return_value = True

        result = auth_service.login("valid-token", unlock_at + 1)
        assert result["success"] is True

    def test_failure_count_resets_after_successful_login(
            self, auth_service, rate_limiter, mock_sso_provider):
        for i in range(3):
            rate_limiter.record_failure(PATIENT_ID, BASE_TIME + i)

        mock_sso_provider.validate_token.return_value = True
        auth_service.login("valid-token", BASE_TIME + 10)
        assert rate_limiter.attempt_count(PATIENT_ID, BASE_TIME + 10) == 0


# ===========================================================================
# TC-SSO-008: Concurrent / Rapid Submissions (stress boundary)
# ===========================================================================

class TestRapidSubmissions:

    @pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
    def force_invalid(self, mock_sso_provider):
        mock_sso_provider.validate_token.return_value = False

    def test_rapid_failures_within_same_second_all_counted(
            self, rate_limiter):
        """All failures at identical timestamp must each be recorded."""
        same_ts = BASE_TIME
        for _ in range(5):
            rate_limiter.record_failure(PATIENT_ID, same_ts)
        assert rate_limiter.attempt_count(PATIENT_ID, same_ts) == 5

    def test_lockout_triggered_on_rapid_same_second_failures(
            self, rate_limiter):
        same_ts = BASE_TIME
        for _ in range(5):
            rate_limiter.record_failure(PATIENT_ID, same_ts)
        assert rate_limiter.is_locked(PATIENT_ID, same_ts) is True

    def test_100_rapid_failures_lockout_persists(self, auth_service, rate_limiter):
        for i in range(100):
            auth_service.login("bad-token", BASE_TIME + i * 0.01)
        # Still locked well within lockout window
        assert rate_limiter.is_locked(PATIENT_ID, BASE_TIME + 2) is True


# ===========================================================================
# TC-SSO-009: Epoch / Calendar Boundary Timestamps
# ===========================================================================

class TestEpochBoundaryTimestamps:

    @pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
    def force_invalid(self, mock_sso_provider):
        mock_sso_provider.validate_token.return_value = False

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("ts,label", [
        (0.0,              "Unix epoch"),
        (1.0,              "1 second after epoch"),
        (86399.0,          "end of first day - 1s"),
        (86400.0,          "start of second day"),
        (2147483647.0,     "Y2K38 boundary (32-bit max)"),
        (2147483648.0,     "Y2K38 + 1s"),
        (1_700_000_000.0,  "representative 2023 timestamp"),
        (9999999999.0,     "far future"),
    ])
    def test_rate_limiter_handles_boundary_timestamp(
            self, rate_limiter, ts, label):
        """Rate limiter must not raise on any valid timestamp."""
        rate_limiter.record_failure(PATIENT_ID, ts)
        count = rate_limiter.attempt_count(PATIENT_ID, ts)
        assert count >= 1, f"Failed for timestamp boundary: {label}"

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("ts", [
        2147483647.0,    # 32-bit signed int max
        2147483648.0,    # overflow boundary
    ])
    def test_lockout_unlock_time_correct_near_y2k38(
            self, rate_limiter, ts):
        LOCKOUT = rate_limiter.LOCKOUT_SECONDS
        for i in range(rate_limiter.MAX_ATTEMPTS):
            rate_limiter.record_failure(PATIENT_ID, ts + i)
        unlock_at = rate_limiter.unlock_time(PATIENT_ID)
        expected = ts + (rate_limiter.MAX_ATTEMPTS - 1) + LOCKOUT
        assert unlock_at == pytest.approx(expected, abs=1)


# ===========================================================================
# TC-SSO-010: Parametrised edge cases
# ===========================================================================

@pytest.mark.parametrize("attempts,expected_locked", [
    (0, False),
    (1, False),
    (2, False),
    (3, False),
    (4, False),   # MAX - 1
    (5, True),    # MAX      ← lockout threshold
    (6, True),    # MAX + 1
    (10, True),
    (100, True),
])
def test_lockout_state_vs_attempt_count(rate_limiter, attempts, expected_locked):
    """Parametrised boundary sweep: lockout state as function of attempt count."""
    for i in range(attempts):
        rate_limiter.record_failure(PATIENT_ID, BASE_TIME + i)
    check_time = BASE_TIME + attempts
    assert rate_limiter.is_locked(PATIENT_ID, check_time) is expected_locked


@pytest.mark.parametrize("window_offset,expected_in_window", [
    (-1,   False),   # 1 second before window open
    (0,    True),    # exactly at window start
    (1,    True),    # 1 second inside window
    (299,  True),    # 1 second before window end
    (300,  True),    # exactly at window end (inclusive)
    (301,  False),   # 1 second past window end
])
def test_window_inclusion_boundary(rate_limiter, window_offset, expected_in_window):
    """Parametrised sweep: which timestamps fall inside the rolling window."""

"""

Requirement: A patient shall log in using SSO; failed attempts shall be
             rate-limited and notify the patient.
             Boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements must be tested.

Author: Quality Engineer
"""

import time
import pytest
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, call
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta
from freezegun import freeze_time

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Assumed application interfaces (replace with real imports in your project)

@pytest.fixture
def mock_sso_provider():

    provider.validate_token.return_value = True
    provider.get_patient_claims.return_value = {

        "email": "patient@example.com",
        "iat": int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),
        "exp": int((datetime.now(timezone.utc) + timedelta(hours=1)).timestamp()),
    }
    return provider


@pytest.fixture

        is_active=True,
        failed_attempts=0,
        locked_until=None,
    )

@pytest.fixture
def rate_limiter(mock_patient_repository):
    """
    In-memory rate limiter stub.
    Replace with your real RateLimiter instantiation.
    """
    from collections import defaultdict

    class InMemoryRateLimiter:
        MAX_ATTEMPTS = 5          # boundary value under test
        WINDOW_SECONDS = 300      # 5-minute rolling window
        LOCKOUT_SECONDS = 900     # 15-minute lockout

        def __init__(self):
            self._attempts: dict = defaultdict(list)  # patient_id -> [timestamp]
            self._lockouts: dict = {}                  # patient_id -> unlock_time

        def record_failure(self, patient_id: str, timestamp: float) -> None:
            self._attempts[patient_id].append(timestamp)
            window_start = timestamp - self.WINDOW_SECONDS
            self._attempts[patient_id] = [
                t for t in self._attempts[patient_id] if t >= window_start
            ]
            if len(self._attempts[patient_id]) >= self.MAX_ATTEMPTS:
                self._lockouts[patient_id] = timestamp + self.LOCKOUT_SECONDS

        def is_locked(self, patient_id: str, current_time: float) -> bool:
            unlock_at = self._lockouts.get(patient_id)
            if unlock_at is None:
                return False
            return current_time < unlock_at

        def unlock_time(self, patient_id: str) -> float | None:
            return self._lockouts.get(patient_id)

        def attempt_count(self, patient_id: str, current_time: float) -> int:
            window_start = current_time - self.WINDOW_SECONDS
            return sum(
                1 for t in self._attempts[patient_id] if t >= window_start
            )

        def reset(self, patient_id: str) -> None:
            self._attempts.pop(patient_id, None)
            self._lockouts.pop(patient_id, None)

    return InMemoryRate
